2026-07-23.explanation: Deuteronomy 6:5 (KJV) - And thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might. (Identified by Jesus in Matthew 22:38 as the first and great commandment).
2026-07-23.opt3: A law for Levitical priests only
2026-07-23.opt4: An ordinance abolished in the New Testament
2026-07-23.question: How did Jesus describe the foundational Old Testament command from Deuteronomy 6:5: 'And thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might'?

2026-07-23.correct: Your heavenly Father will also forgive you
2026-07-23.explanation: Matthew 6:14 (KJV) - For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you:
2026-07-23.opt1: You will receive honor from society
2026-07-23.opt2: Your heavenly Father will also forgive you
2026-07-23.opt3: Your enemies will be made slaves
2026-07-23.question: In the Sermon on the Mount, what spiritual promise did Jesus give to those who forgive men their trespasses?

2026-07-23.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:10 (KJV) - But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:
2026-07-23.question: According to 2 Timothy 1:10, our Saviour Jesus Christ has abolished death and brought what two things to light through the gospel?

2026-07-23.explanation: Habakkuk 2:4 (KJV) - Behold, his soul which is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith. (Quoted in Romans 1:17, Galatians 3:11, Hebrews 10:38).
2026-07-23.question: In which Minor Prophet's Old Testament book do we find the famous declaration quoted multiple times in the New Testament: 'The just shall live by his faith'?

2026-07-23.explanation: Daniel 6:10 (KJV) - Now when Daniel knew that the writing was signed, he went into his house; and his windows being open in his chamber toward Jerusalem, he kneeled upon his knees three times a day, and prayed, and gave thanks before his God, as he did aforetime.
2026-07-23.question: Despite the royal decree forbidding prayer to anyone save King Darius, how many times a day did Daniel kneel by his open windows toward Jerusalem to pray and give thanks to his God?

2026-07-23.correct: According to his own purpose and grace
2026-07-23.explanation: 2 Timothy 1:9 (KJV) - Who hath saved us, and called us with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,
2026-07-23.opt1: According to our works of righteousness
2026-07-23.opt2: According to his own purpose and grace
2026-07-23.question: According to 2 Timothy 1:9, God has saved us and called us with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to what?

2026-07-23.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:12 (KJV) - Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.
2026-07-23.question: 'Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.' To which young coworker did Paul write these exhortations?

2026-07-23.explanation: 1 Corinthians 12:4-11 (KJV) - Now there are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit... the manifestation of the Spirit is given to every man to profit withal (word of wisdom, word of knowledge, faith, gifts of healing, etc.).
2026-07-23.question: The word of wisdom, gifts of healing, working of miracles, prophecy, and divers kinds of tongues described by Paul in 1 Corinthians chapter 12 are collectively categorized as what?

2026-07-23.correct: By taking heed thereto according to thy word
2026-07-23.explanation: Psalms 119:9 (KJV) - Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? by taking heed thereto according to thy word.
2026-07-23.opt2: By taking heed thereto according to thy word
2026-07-23.question: In Psalm 119:9, how does the psalmist answer the question: 'Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way?'

2026-07-24.explanation: Philippians 3:20 (KJV) - For our conversation is in heaven; from whence also we look for the Saviour, the Lord Jesus Christ:
2026-07-24.question: According to Philippians chapter 3, where is our conversation (citizenship), from whence also we look for the Saviour, the Lord Jesus Christ?

2026-07-24.correct: Shall cover the multitude of sins
2026-07-24.explanation: 1 Peter 4:8 (KJV) - And above all things have fervent charity among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins.
2026-07-24.opt2: Shall cover the multitude of sins
2026-07-24.question: Why does Peter exhort believers in 1 Peter chapter 4 to have fervent charity (love) among themselves above all things?

2026-07-24.explanation: Micah 7:18 (KJV) - Who is a God like unto thee, that pardoneth iniquity, and passeth by the transgression of the remnant of his heritage? he retaineth not his anger for ever, because he delighteth in mercy.
2026-07-24.question: 'Who is a God like unto thee, that pardoneth iniquity, and passeth by the transgression of the remnant of his heritage?' Which Old Testament prophet penned this hymn of praise to God's forgiving mercy?

2026-07-24.explanation: 1 Peter 1:24-25 (KJV) - For all flesh is as grass... The grass withereth, and the flower thereof falleth away: But the word of the Lord endureth for ever. And this is the word which by the gospel is preached unto you.
2026-07-24.question: While all flesh is as grass that withereth and falleth away, how long does 1 Peter chapter 1 state the word of the Lord (which by the gospel is preached unto us) endure?

2026-07-24.explanation: Luke 7:9 (KJV) - When Jesus heard these things, he marvelled at him, and turned him about, and said unto the people that followed him, I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
2026-07-24.question: Who declared to Jesus, 'Speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed,' prompting Christ to marvel and say He had not found so great faith, no, not in Israel?

2026-07-24.explanation: Matthew 26:36, 41 (KJV) - Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane... Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
2026-07-24.question: In what specific location on the night before His crucifixion did Jesus warn His sleeping disciples: 'Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation'?

2026-07-24.explanation: Acts 15:11 (KJV) - But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they.
2026-07-24.question: During the theological debate regarding Gentile believers, who stood up in the Jerusalem Council and declared, 'But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they'?

2026-07-24.explanation: Romans 12:1 (KJV) - I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
2026-07-24.question: According to Paul's appeal in Romans 12:1, how are brethren besought to present their bodies unto God as holy and acceptable?

2026-07-24.explanation: John 16:13 (KJV) - Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself...
2026-07-24.question: When the Spirit of truth is come, into what did Jesus promise He will guide believers according to John chapter 16?

2026-07-24.explanation: Ecclesiastes 12:13 (KJV) - Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty of man.
2026-07-24.question: 'Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty of man.' In which Old Testament book is this wise conclusion recorded?

2026-07-25.explanation: Titus 2:13 (KJV) - Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;
2026-07-25.question: What phrase does Paul use in Titus chapter 2 to describe the joyful anticipation of the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ?

2026-07-25.explanation: Song of Solomon 8:6 (KJV) - Set me as a seal upon thine heart, as a seal upon thine arm: for love is strong as death...
2026-07-25.question: 'For love is strong as death... the coals thereof are coals of fire, which hath a most vehement flame.' In which poetic Old Testament book is this passionate description of love found?

2026-07-25.explanation: Acts 13:38 (KJV) - Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins:
2026-07-25.question: 'Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins.' Who preached this gospel message in the synagogue at Antioch in Pisidia?

2026-07-25.correct: Sealed with that holy Spirit of promise
2026-07-25.explanation: Ephesians 1:13 (KJV) - In whom ye also trusted, after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise,
2026-07-25.opt2: Sealed with that holy Spirit of promise
2026-07-25.question: According to Ephesians 1:13, after believers hear the word of truth, the gospel of their salvation, and believe in Christ, with what divine seal are they marked?

2026-07-25.explanation: Romans 4:3 (KJV) - For what saith the scripture? Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness. (Genesis 15:6).
2026-07-25.question: Concerning which patriarch often called the father of the faithful does Scripture declare: 'He believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness'?

2026-07-25.explanation: Nehemiah 1:4 (KJV) - And it came to pass, when I heard these words, that I sat down and wept, and mourned certain days, and fasted, and prayed before the God of heaven,
2026-07-25.question: Which Persian royal cupbearer sat down, wept, fasted, and prayed before the God of heaven upon hearing that Jerusalem's walls were broken down and its gates burned with fire?

2026-07-25.explanation: Zechariah 4:7 (KJV) - Who art thou, O great mountain? before Zerubbabel thou shalt become a plain: and he shall bring forth the headstone thereof with shoutings, crying, Grace, grace unto it.
2026-07-25.question: 'Who art thou, O great mountain?... thou shalt become a plain: and he shall bring forth the headstone thereof with shoutings, crying, Grace, grace unto it.' Before which leader did God prophesy the mountain would become a plain?

2026-07-25.explanation: Isaiah 6:2-3 (KJV) - Above it stood the seraphims... And one cried unto another, and said, Holy, holy, holy, is the LORD of hosts: the whole earth is full of his glory.
2026-07-25.question: In Isaiah's heavenly vision of God's throne, which order of angelic beings stood above it crying one to another, 'Holy, holy, holy, is the LORD of hosts'?

2026-07-25.explanation: Joel 2:28 (KJV) - And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy... (Quoted by Peter on Pentecost in Acts 2:16-17).
2026-07-25.question: 'And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy...' Which Old Testament prophet uttered this famous prophecy of the outpouring of the Holy Ghost?

2026-07-25.correct: Have right to the tree of life
2026-07-25.explanation: Revelation 22:14 (KJV) - Blessed are they that do his commandments, that they may have right to the tree of life, and may enter in through the gates into the city.
2026-07-25.opt2: Have right to the tree of life
2026-07-25.question: According to Revelation 22:14, blessed are they that do His commandments, that they may enter through the gates into the city and have right to what heavenly tree?
